title: Switch & Ternary 🚦
description: Shorter, cleaner ways to write logic when you have many paths.

slides.title: Tired of Typing? 🥱
slides.content:
# If-Else Fatigue

If you have a dozen `else if` statements checking the exact same variable over and over, your code gets long and ugly.

Whenever you want to check one variable against many possible EXACT values, use a `switch` statement instead!

slides.title: Switch Statement 🚂

quizData.question: In a switch statement, what acts like the final 'else' block?
quizData.explanation: The 'default:' case catches any values that didn't match the specific cases above it.

slides.title: The Ternary Operator ?: 😎
slides.content:
## The Mini-If

Sometimes, an `if / else` statement is too bulky for a simple True/False task.

Dart has a shortcut called the **Ternary Operator**.
Format: `Condition ? WhatIfTrue : WhatIfFalse;`

```dart
int age = 15;
String title = age >= 18 ? 'Adult' : 'Child';
```
It checks `age >= 18`. If true, it returns 'Adult'. If false, it returns 'Child'.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Once you learn Ternary, you'll love it!

quizData.explanation: The ternary operator uses ? directly after the condition.
